incident_summary: API gateway experienced severe latency degradation (p99 15.2s vs normal <1s) for approximately 2.5 hours due to database connection pool exhaustion caused by a configuration change that tripled HTTP client timeout values.

root_cause.summary: HTTP client timeout configuration change from 10s to 30s caused database connection pool exhaustion by holding connections 3x longer than normal, preventing connection recycling.
root_cause.trigger: Deployment at 2025-01-15T14:15:00Z changing HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s
root_cause.mechanism: Tripled timeout duration prevented connection pool from recycling connections fast enough; when all 50 connections became occupied, new requests queued for up to 30s causing cascading latency and timeouts

customer_impact.description: Customers experienced severe API performance degradation with response times increasing from under 1 second to over 15 seconds, causing timeouts and intermittent errors when accessing the platform.

resolution.action_taken: Rolled back the HTTP client timeout configuration change, reverting timeout from 30s back to 10s
resolution.confirmed_by: Connection pool utilization dropped from 100% (50/50) to 60% (30/50) per CloudWatch logs at 15:05 UTC; engineer monitoring confirmed metrics stable for 90 minutes post-rollback before resolving incident

root_cause: GitHub deployment showing PR #12345 deployed at 14:15 UTC changing timeout 10s→30s to api-gateway
Slack thread engineer confirmation: 'Found it! PR #12345 deployed at 2:15 PM. Changed HTTP client timeout from 10s to 30s. That's holding connections 3x longer!'
Slack thread: 'Connection pool can't recycle fast enough with the longer timeout'
GitHub deployment showing rollback PR #12347 at 15:45 UTC with description 'Emergency rollback of PR #12345 due to database connection pool exhaustion'
CloudWatch logs showing recovery at 15:05 UTC after rollback
timeline: GitHub deployment timestamp 14:15 UTC for causal change
customer_impact: PagerDuty alert showing p99 latency 15.2s vs threshold 5s
CloudWatch logs showing 30s connection wait times
Connection timeout errors would manifest as API errors/timeouts to end users

data_gaps: Geographic scope unknown - no data indicates whether impact was global or regional

Baseline metrics incomplete - no pre-incident p99 latency values provided to quantify degradation magnitude
Other services/products status unknown - only api-gateway mentioned, unclear if other services were affected
Customer reports/tickets not included - impact assessment based on technical metrics only
